# @opencanon/canon — LLM Reference

opencanon is a fiction canon protocol. A canon is a novel whose structure lives in a GitHub repo, validated for consistency, with every episode committed immutably. The CLI handles local scaffolding, AI-assisted writing, compliance checking, and publishing.

---

## Onboarding (guide users through this)

```
1. canon setup          → interactive wizard (title, protagonist, genre, synopsis)
2. canon login          → paste token from opencanon.co/settings
3. canon write ep01     → scaffold or generate first episode
4. canon push           → check + lock + commit + push + publish
```

---

## Commands

```
canon setup [dir]                  Scaffold novel: canon/, stories/, .canonrc.json
canon login [--host <url>]         Save oct_... token to ~/.canon/config.json
canon write <slug> [options]       Collect refs + scaffold or generate episode
  --generate                       Call web app AI → stream prose to chapter-01.md
  --direction <text>               Writing direction hint (default: "continue naturally")
  --no-refs                        Skip cross-canon references
  --no-notebook                    Skip notebook context
canon push [dir] [options]         check → lock → git commit+push → publish
  --message <msg>                  Git commit message (auto-generated if omitted)
  --no-publish                     Skip publish after push
  --dry-run                        Show steps without executing
canon check [dir]                  Run 7 compliance checks. Exit 0 = pass
canon lock [dir]                   Regenerate canon.lock.json
canon publish [dir]                Register on opencanon.co
canon new story <id>               Create stories/<id>/metadata.json
canon new character <id>           Create canon/characters/<id>/definition.json
canon new location <id>            Create canon/worldbuilding/locations/<id>.json
```

---

## canon write — 3 reference sources

| Source | What | Limit |
|---|---|---|
| ① self | Latest chapter from this repo | 800 chars |
| ② notebook | Writer's notes from opencanon.co/notebook | 600 chars |
| ③ cross | Snippets from up to 3 other registered novels | 200 chars each |

Each source is SHA-256 hashed (12 chars) and saved to `.canon-refs.json`. Cross-referenced novels receive an attestation. With `--generate`, refs are sent to `/api/cli/generate` → prose streamed back.

---

## 7 compliance checks (canon check)

```
metadata_schema_valid   Required fields, schema_version === "1.2"
characters_valid        characters[] IDs exist in canon/characters/
locations_valid         locations[] IDs exist in canon/worldbuilding/locations/
timeline_consistent     timeline is YYYY-MM-DD
continuity_valid        prev/next/thematic_echoes resolve
canon_version_match     canon_ref matches canon.lock.json
contributor_valid       contributor field non-empty
```

---

## metadata.json (v1.2)

```json
{
  "schema_version": "1.2",
  "canon_ref": "<commit-sha>",
  "id": "<episode-slug>",
  "episode": 1,
  "title": { "ko": "...", "en": "..." },
  "timeline": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "synopsis": { "ko": "...", "en": "..." },
  "characters": ["character-id"],
  "locations": ["location-id"],
  "contributor": "<github-username>",
  "canon_status": "canonical"
}
```

---

## Repo structure

```
canon/
  characters/{id}/definition.json
  worldbuilding/locations/{id}.json
stories/{slug}/
  metadata.json
  chapter-01.md
.canonrc.json
canon.lock.json
.canon-refs.json        ← auto-generated by canon write
```

---

## Errors

```
INVALID_TOKEN      → canon login (regenerate at /settings)
NOT_REGISTERED     → register repo at opencanon.co first
FORBIDDEN          → token owner ≠ repo owner
canon check exit 1 → fix reported field in metadata.json
```

---

## Web app endpoints used by CLI

```
GET  /api/notebook          → notebook context for canon write
GET  /api/registry          → cross-canon list for canon write
POST /api/attest            → attest cross-referenced novel
POST /api/publish           → register/update canon
POST /api/cli/generate      → SSE stream: generate episode prose
```

Default host: `https://opencanon.co` — override with `--host`
